name: large-file-gate.js
purpose: PreToolUse gate (Read) that blocks reads of .jsonl/.log/.csv/.ndjson files >5MB and warns on files >500KB to prevent context waste; exits 2 to block, 0 to allow.
notes: SKIP_GATES=1 kill switch. Resolves symlinks via realpathSync before traversal guard. Appends to hook-warnings-log.jsonl on block/warn (best-effort, never blocks). Fail-open on payload >1MB, stdin errors, parse errors. Per D20/D13 internal refs. Step 15 hook-if-implementation plan.

name: loop-detector.js
purpose: PostToolUseFailure hook (Bash) that detects repeated identical build/test failures by hashing normalized error text; warns Claude after 3+ occurrences within a 20-minute rolling window.
notes: FIRST PostToolUseFailure hook in the project. Always exits 0 — warns, never blocks. Error normalization strips line:col numbers, ISO timestamps, Unix epochs, temp paths, and ANSI escapes before SHA-256 hashing. State written atomically (tmp+rename). Capped rolling window of 20 min. continueOnError: true as backup. Per D12 internal ref. Step 11 hook-if-implementation plan.
if_condition: Bash(npm run build *)|Bash(npm test *)|Bash(npx tsc *)|Bash(npm run lint *)

name: post-read-handler.js
purpose: Consolidated PostToolUse (Read) hook that (1) tracks files read per session and warns when >5000-line file is read without pagination or >15 files read total; (2) auto-saves context to .claude/pending-mcp-save.json for MCP memory when 20+ files read and 15+ minutes since last save.
notes: Session #164 consolidation of 3 PostToolUse Read hooks (large-context-warning.js + auto-save-context.js) into 1 process. Phase 3 (compaction-handoff) was removed — pre-compaction-save.js is authoritative. Uses argv[2] JSON parse (not stdin). Calls process.chdir(projectDir). Reads docs/SESSION_DECISIONS.md for getRecentDecisions() — SoNash-specific path. MCP memory entity name includes date. Always exits 0.

name: post-todos-render.js
purpose: PostToolUse hook (Write/Edit/MultiEdit) that fires when .planning/todos.jsonl is modified; re-renders .planning/TODOS.md via scripts/planning/render-todos.js and stages the result so JSONL and MD are always in sync.
notes: Three separate PostToolUse wiring entries cover Write/Edit/MultiEdit on .planning/todos.jsonl with continueOnError: true. Non-blocking on renderer failure. Uses execFileSync (process.execPath, not bare 'node' — PR #420 exec-path rule). Stages TODOS.md with git add after successful render. isCanonicalTodosPath() uses realpathSync + symlink rejection for containment check. writeAudit() requires safe-fs — silently skips if unavailable. Module exports testable functions. PR #517 R1 propagation — refuse-symlink pattern.

name: post-write-validator.js
purpose: Consolidated PostToolUse hook (Write/Edit/MultiEdit) running 10 validators in sequence: firestoreWriteBlock (BLOCK), testMockingValidator (BLOCK), auditS0S1 (WARN/BLOCK), patternCheck (WARN), componentSizeCheck (WARN), appCheckValidator (WARN), typescriptStrictCheck (WARN), repositoryPatternCheck (WARN), markdownFenceCheck (WARN), jsonSyntaxCheck (WARN), agentTriggerEnforcer (SUGGEST), testRegistryReminder (SUGGEST).
notes: Replaces 10 separate hooks — ~800ms saved on Windows per comment. Three settings.json wiring entries (Write, Edit, MultiEdit) with CLAUDE_TOOL env var set. Validators run blocking-first then WARN. agentTriggerEnforcer has phased rollout (1=SUGGEST, 2=WARN, 3=BLOCK). AUDIT_S0S1_MODE env controls WARN vs BLOCK for auditS0S1. appCheckValidator gated by APP_CHECK_ENABLED env or .agent-trigger-state.json. testRegistryReminder references SoNash-specific data/ecosystem-v2/test-registry.jsonl. Exit: 'block: reason' to stdout OR 'ok' to stdout. Not portable as-is — deeply Firebase/SoNash-coupled.

name: pre-commit-agent-compliance.js
purpose: PreToolUse gate (Bash) that blocks git commits when staged code files exist but code-reviewer was not invoked, or security-sensitive files are staged but security-auditor was not invoked; reads .session-agents.json for invocation history.
notes: Session #226 Decision #27 — strict mode for POST-TASK triggers. Exits 2 to block. --no-verify bypasses check (pass-through). 3s stdin timeout + 15s overall timeout. Reads staged files via git diff --cached. SECURITY_PATTERNS includes firestore.rules and functions/src/ — these are SoNash-specific and must be replaced for JASON-OS port. Agent names (code-reviewer, security-auditor) are also project-tunable.

name: pre-compaction-save.js
purpose: PreCompact hook that captures a comprehensive session snapshot to .claude/state/handoff.json immediately before context compaction; includes session counter, task states, recent commits, git context, agent invocations, files read, active plan, session notes, and active ecosystem audits.
notes: Session #138 — part of compaction-resilient state persistence. Fires on PreCompact (NEW event vs JASON-OS). Uses $ARGUMENTS to detect trigger type. gatherTeamStatus() checks CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS env var. gatherActiveAudits() scans .claude/tmp/ for *-audit-progress.json (SoNash ecosystem audit pattern). Reads SESSION_CONTEXT.md for session counter — format-specific. Always exits 0. Output via saveJson (state-utils.js). Companion: compact-restore.js (D3a) reads the handoff.json on next SessionStart.

name: session-start.js
purpose: SessionStart hook that orchestrates 15+ startup tasks: npm install (root + functions, lockfile-hash-cached), Firebase Functions build, test compilation, session state management (previous-session end check), secrets status, hook-warnings.json regeneration from JSONL, 5-stage enforcement pipeline (lifecycle gaps, enforcement gaps, scaffold refinement, verify enforcement, ratchet baselines), TDMS health check, health score delta, todo summary, and CLI tool detection from tool-manifest.json.
notes: Largest SessionStart hook — 1350 lines. Deeply SoNash-specific: Firebase Functions build, review-lifecycle.js, TDMS metrics, SoNash enforcement pipeline. Not portable as-is. The warning regeneration system (D16 architecture: JSONL canonical source + ack state -> regenerated hook-warnings.json view) IS the portable pattern but the specific scripts are SoNash. v1/v2 pipeline coexistence note in header (INTG-06). RATCHET_BASELINES_TIMEOUT_MS env var override for slow Windows machines. Uses execSync (not execFileSync) for npm commands — flags this as a minor gap vs strict standards.

name: settings-guardian.js
purpose: PreToolUse gate (Write/Edit) that blocks modifications to .claude/settings.json which would remove critical hooks (block-push-to-main.js, pre-commit-agent-compliance.js, settings-guardian.js itself); validates full JSON structure on Write, checks old_string/new_string on Edit.
sanitize_fields: CRITICAL_HOOKS list (project-specific hook names to protect)
notes: SKIP_GATES=1 kill switch. CRITICAL_HOOKS hardcodes 3 hook names — must be updated for JASON-OS. Deep-walks entire JSON tree to find hook references (containsHookReference). Appends warning via execFileSync to scripts/append-hook-warning.js (synchronous, not async_spawn). Session #245 Step 8. Self-referential: protects itself in CRITICAL_HOOKS list.

name: test-tracker.js
purpose: PostToolUse hook (Bash) that captures test run results (pass/fail + exit code) to .claude/state/test-runs.jsonl; on failure also appends a warning to hook-warnings-log.jsonl via append-hook-warning.js.
notes: Always exits 0 — tracker, never blocks. continueOnError: true as backup. TEST_COMMAND_REGEX: npm test, npx vitest, npx jest, npm run test. Rotates test-runs.jsonl: cap 200, keep 150. Appends failure warning via execFileSync to scripts/append-hook-warning.js (synchronous). SESSION_CONTEXT.md counter dependency graceful on absence. Per D16 internal ref. Step 14 hook-if-implementation plan.

name: track-agent-invocation.js
purpose: PostToolUse hook (Task/Agent tool) that records each subagent_type invocation to .claude/hooks/.session-agents.json (session window, reset on session ID change) and appends a persistent record to .claude/state/agent-invocations.jsonl; data used by pre-commit-agent-compliance.js and pre-compaction-save.js.
notes: Session #101. Reads stdin directly via fs.readFileSync(0) instead of event listener pattern — distinguishes it from most other hooks. Description sanitization: truncates to 100 chars, redacts base64-like tokens, emails, and sensitive keywords. Session reset only on genuine session ID change. Rotate agent-invocations.jsonl at >32KB (keep 100). Uses withLock from safe-fs for append+rotate race prevention. Always exits 0.

name: user-prompt-handler.js
purpose: UserPromptSubmit hook (718 lines) that runs 6 analysis phases on each user prompt: (1) alerts reminder with 10-min cooldown; (2) keyword-based agent/skill directive injection (security-auditor, systematic-debugging, database-architect, frontend-design); (3) session-end detection with 60-min cooldown; (4) multi-step task complexity detection; (5) always-on guardrails injection; (6) frustration/correction detection (HARD STOP).
sanitize_fields: Agent/skill names in directives (security-auditor, systematic-debugging, database-architect, frontend-design — project-tunable)
GUARDRAILS text body (project-specific CLAUDE.md section refs)
notes: NEW event vs JASON-OS: UserPromptSubmit. Reads stdin via fs.readFileSync(0) (same pattern as track-agent-invocation.js). Prompt capped at 2000 chars. Directive dedup: 15-min window per directive. Suggest dedup: 4-hour session window. Frustration detector: ALL CAPS at sentence start (3+ words), correction phrases, repeated punct + negative sentiment. HARD STOP directive outputs to stdout for Claude context injection. Guardrails text injected on every non-trivial prompt (~63 tokens/turn). SoNash-specific: 'firestore' keyword in database-architect trigger, /session-end skill reference, agent names.

name: git-utils.js
purpose: Shared git utility library for hooks: resolves and validates project directory from CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env (with ancestor/descendant containment check), provides gitExec() wrapper for synchronous git commands with error-silent fallback.
notes: Exports: { gitExec, projectDir }. projectDir is resolved at module load time — side effect on require(). Depth limit: rejects CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR ancestors more than 10 levels above cwd. Windows-aware: uses path.sep and case-insensitive normalization. Callers: post-read-handler.js, post-write-validator.js, pre-compaction-save.js, pre-commit-agent-compliance.js. Used by D3a hooks: commit-tracker.js, compact-restore.js.

name: inline-patterns.js
purpose: Pattern library for post-write-validator.js: 15 inline pattern definitions (npm-install-automation, exit-code-capture, exec-no-global-flag, race-condition-existssync, unvalidated-redirect, console-log-in-production, any-type-usage, todo-without-ticket, unsafe-json-parse, hardcoded-localhost, empty-catch-block, xss-dangerouslysetinnerhtml, unrestricted-cors, missing-error-boundary) plus checkInlinePatterns() runner.
sanitize_fields: pathExclude patterns (SoNash-specific dir names like scripts/, .claude/, etc. are generic enough but should be reviewed)
console-log-in-production pathExclude (scripts, .claude, tests) — adjust per project
notes: NOTE: Many JS/TS patterns were migrated from this file to ESLint AST rules in eslint-plugin-sonash (v3.0). Remaining patterns cover: bash/YAML patterns (npm-install-automation, exit-code-capture), not-yet-ESLint-covered patterns, and fast inline feedback patterns. Exports: { INLINE_PATTERNS, checkInlinePatterns }. Caller: post-write-validator.js only. Pattern runner handles /g flag enforcement (no infinite-loop risk) and per-match line number tracking.

name: rotate-state.js
purpose: Shared state file rotation library providing: rotateJsonl (cap JSONL to N entries), pruneJsonKey (prune array field in JSON), expireByAge (expire JSON object entries by date), expireJsonlByAge (expire JSONL entries by timestamp field), archiveRotateJsonl (rotate with archive + ack-aware eviction + advisory file locking).
notes: Session #160 Wave 2 AI Optimization Audit. archiveRotateJsonl: ack-aware rotation — preserves unacked entries preferentially; uses withLock from safe-fs for locking; appends evicted entries to .archive file. All atomic writes via tmp+rename. pruneJsonKey supports dot-separated key paths. Callers: commit-tracker.js (D3a), test-tracker.js, track-agent-invocation.js, session-start.js, and pre-compaction-save.js indirectly via state-utils.js.

name: sanitize-input.js
purpose: Shared input sanitization library for hooks: sanitizeInput() strips control characters (except tab/newline/CR), redacts patterns matching SECRET_PATTERNS (tokens 24+ chars, bearer/basic auth, key=value with sensitive names), and truncates to configurable maxLength (default 500).
notes: Exports: { sanitizeInput, SECRET_PATTERNS }. SECRET_PATTERNS: 5 regex patterns. Uses charCodeAt loop instead of regex for control char filtering (avoids no-control-regex lint). Callers: post-write-validator.js, track-agent-invocation.js, session-start.js, user-prompt-handler.js (imports isSafeToWrite only, not sanitizeInput). Inline fallback in many hooks uses similar but simpler pattern.

name: state-utils.js
purpose: Shared JSON state persistence library: loadJson() (parse JSON file, null on error), saveJson() (atomic write with tmp+backup strategy, symlink guard, Windows cross-drive fallback via direct write).
notes: Exports: { loadJson, saveJson }. backupSwap(): moves existing dest to .bak, renames tmp to dest; restores backup on failure. On Windows cross-drive rename failure, falls back to direct writeFileSync. isSafeToWrite fallback (if symlink-guard unavailable) restricts writes to .claude/state/ and .claude/hooks/ subdirectories. Callers: post-read-handler.js, pre-compaction-save.js, compact-restore.js (D3a).

name: symlink-guard.js
purpose: Shared symlink guard for hook state file writes: isSafeToWrite() walks the file path and all ancestor directories, returning false if any is a symlink or if the check fails (fail-closed).
notes: Exports: { isSafeToWrite }. Only exported function. Rejects relative paths (requires absolute). Fail-closed on any exception. Used by nearly every hook that writes state files. Also inlined/reimplemented in: gsd-check-update.js, rotate-state.js (inline fallback), state-utils.js (inline fallback). Most widely-used lib helper in the hooks system.

name: gsd-check-update.js
purpose: SessionStart global hook that spawns a detached background process to check npm for a newer version of the get-shit-done-cc package; writes result to ~/.claude/cache/gsd-update-check.json.
notes: GSD-specific hook — checks for updates to the get-shit-done-cc npm package. Reads ~/.claude/get-shit-done/VERSION (machine-scoped). Writes to ~/.claude/cache/ (machine-scoped). async_spawn: true — spawn with detached:true + child.unref(). The spawned child runs inline JS string (not a separate script file). Inline symlink guard duplicated from symlink-guard.js. Not wired via event in the hooks section proper — wired as third SessionStart entry in settings.json (no matcher). Will need to check if this fires on ALL sessions or just GSD sessions. Not portable to JASON-OS.

name: statusline.js
purpose: GSD statusline hook (not wired in settings.json hooks section, but present in hooks/global/): reads Claude Code session JSON from stdin and renders model | branch | current task | directory | context window bar to stdout.
notes: NOT wired in .claude/hooks or settings.json hooks section — wired via settings.json statusLine config (separate from hooks). GSD-edition statusline. Reads todo files from ~/.claude/todos/ (machine-scoped). findCurrentTask() reads session-specific todo files from ~/.claude/todos/ directory. sanitize() strips control chars and ANSI escapes from all dynamic values. Context window bar: 10-block ASCII art with color thresholds (green <50%, yellow <65%, orange <80%, blinking red >=80%). SoNash has its own versioned statusline binary (sonash-statusline-v3.exe) — this is the GSD global JS version. Not portable.
event: (not a hook event — wired via settings.json statusLine.command, not hooks section)
